meta.title: Graph Signal Lab
meta.dataset_filter: top 600 positively rated movies; users with at least 8 positive ratings in the subset
meta.citation: F. Maxwell Harper and Joseph A. Konstan. 2015. The MovieLens Datasets: History and Context.
meta.note: Scores are generated by browser-side LightGCN inference from these learned embeddings and graph edges.

scenarios.title: Held-out like
scenarios.lesson: This is a real held-out MovieLens rating. The edge is not in the training graph; the score comes from learned embeddings and neighboring movie/genre structure.

nodes.label: U425
nodes.name: MovieLens user 425
nodes.description: Target MovieLens user. The id is anonymized by the dataset.

nodes.label: Action
nodes.name: Action
nodes.description: Genre node connected from MovieLens movie metadata.

nodes.label: Crime
nodes.name: Crime
nodes.description: Genre node connected from MovieLens movie metadata.

nodes.label: Drama
nodes.name: Drama
nodes.description: Genre node connected from MovieLens movie metadata.

scenarios.prompt: Predict whether anonymized MovieLens user 425 will like Inception (2010). The held-out rating was 4.5 stars.

scenarios.title: One-hop lift
scenarios.lesson: The trained graph convolution quickly raises this held-out like once nearby users, movies, and genres start sharing signal.

nodes.name: Sci-Fi
nodes.description: Genre node connected from MovieLens movie metadata.

scenarios.prompt: Predict whether anonymized MovieLens user 325 will like Ghostbusters (a.k.a. Ghost Busters) (1984). The held-out rating was 4 stars.

scenarios.title: False alarm
scenarios.lesson: This is an unrated user-movie pair sampled as a negative. The same trained model can lower a tempting candidate when graph context does not support it.

nodes.label: Romance
nodes.name: Romance
nodes.description: Genre node connected from MovieLens movie metadata.

scenarios.prompt: Predict whether anonymized MovieLens user 227 should get Sense and Sensibility (1995). This sampled pair has no positive rating in the dataset.

scenarios.title: Oversmoothing check
scenarios.lesson: The model was trained for two-hop inference. Sweeping deeper layers is literal inference too, but extra propagation can wash out the pair-specific signal.
